title: Source all secrets from a CSPRNG
description: Generate keys, salts, nonces, and tokens from a cryptographically secure RNG, never from a general-purpose PRNG.
body: Use whenever a value must be unpredictable: keys, salts, IVs/nonces, session tokens, password-reset codes. Do not use a language's default PRNG (Math.random, rand(), Mersenne Twister) for any secret, and avoid seeding from time or PID. Actions: call the platform CSPRNG (getrandom, crypto.randomBytes, SecureRandom, libsodium randombytes) and request enough bytes for the target security level. Validation signals include a code path where every secret traces to a CSPRNG and a test rejecting non-CSPRNG sources. Risk boundary: predictable randomness lets attackers reproduce keys and tokens, defeating the whole scheme; treat any secret derived from a non-cryptographic RNG as a critical defect.
